channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hiding kids' 'gender identity' from parents is common in blue state fighting Trump on trans issues: watchdog
item.description: A watchdog group that tracks gender ideology in schools found more than 50 school districts in Maine have policies that exclude parents from learning their children have gender dysphoria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the US-Mexico border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the U.S.-Mexico border as President Donald Trump seeks to clamp down on illegal immigration and fulfill a central promise of his campaign, U.S. officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-NFLer Chris Kluwe says he was fired from job after MAGA protest
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe said he was fired from his high school football coaching position after a MAGA plaque protest.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: What's the point of all these anti-DOGE lawsuits? Fight Trump's agenda to SCOTUS, legal experts say
item.description: Legal experts point out that as anti-Trump plaintiffs seek to stifle his agenda, courts are navigating uncharted waters when it comes to DOGE spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frustrations mount as Stefanik stuck in limbo
item.description: Frustrations are growing as Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) wait to be confirmed as ambassador to the United Nations could stretch until April due to the House’s ultra-slim majority. Stefanik is facing a unique problem. Despite numerous international crises facing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees are starting to receive another email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians won't stop fighting, even if US pulls support: Veteran
item.description: Carl Larson of Seattle fought on Ukraine’s front lines for six months, an experience he describes as “horrific.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first time he spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, President Donald Trump tried to pressure Ukraine's new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election. It was a 2019 phone call that eventually sparked an impeachment.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California lawmaker revives AI whistleblower protections concerns in new bill
item.description: The California lawmaker who introduced an AI safety protection bill later vetoed by Gov. Gavin Newsom has again introduced legislation to regulate the powerful technology. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: European leaders pledge to stand by Ukraine after confrontational Oval Office meeting with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump's order making English the official language in the US could mean
item.description: As President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, activists and advocacy groups are alarmed by what that will mean for non-English speakers when it comes to immigration, voter access and other issues.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Trump, Zelensky meeting went off the rails
item.description: Efforts to negotiate an end to the war in Ukraine were left in tatters following President Trump’s confrontational White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, which erupted into one of the most remarkable Oval Office scenes in years.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's top prosecutor in DC demotes several supervisors who handled politically sensitive cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Several senior leaders in Washington's federal prosecutors office have been demoted to jobs handling misdemeanors or other low-level matters, in the latest move by President Donald Trump's U.S. attorney to roil the office that oversaw the massive prosecution of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and BYRON TAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Those with gender dysphoria can no longer serve in military: Pentagon
item.description: (NewsNation) — A memo sent to Defense Department leaders Thursday states those with gender dysphoria are no longer eligible for military service. This document, written by Darin S.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of weather forecasters fired in latest wave of DOGE cuts
item.description: Hundreds of weather forecasters and other federal National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration employees on probationary status have been fired, lawmakers and weather experts say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Behold the beaver! Drive is on to make prehistoric giant Minnesota’s official fossil
item.description: Minnesota is among a handful of states that doesn’t have an official fossil among its symbols. The Minnesota Science Museum is hoping to change that.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Nevada film tax bill would bring jobs, but questions remain as lawmakers examine details
item.description: Lawmakers dug into details of the film tax credit bill on Thursday, with film studio executives from Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony answering questions in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illegal marijuana taking a big bite out of Nevada tax revenue, school funding
item.description: The illicit market for cannabis in Nevada has grown to a $242 million business, taking a bite out of the state's effort to profit from the legalization of marijuana.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UNLV testing cameras to read license plates in aftermath of deadly 2023 attack
item.description: Cameras that can read license plates are part of the security response that followed the Dec. 5, 2023, shooting on the UNLV campus. They are just part of what UNLV is doing in an effort to keep students safer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
